p.seg: To all who shall see or hear this written deed, Walter son of Alan, steward of the lord king of Scotland, sends greeting in the Lord.

Let your whole community know that the land of Mow with its correct boundaries and with whatever is contained within the same boundaries that I held in feu of the lord William de Vescy I have given in exchange to the monks of Melrose in return for a land that is called ‘Freretun’, with all its pertinents, everything that the nuns of South Berwick possessed in that place; and in addition, together with the same land which is called ‘Freretun’, the same monks have given me two hundred merks by reason of the same exchange. I and my heirs shall warrant, defend and maintain this land of Mow with its contents and its pertinents within its boundaries. As witness of this matter I have reinforced the present written deed by the application of my seal.

With these witnesses: Thomas Crook, Simon son of Bertolf, Adam Wallace, William of Halkerston, Robert of Kippo, knights; William and Robert, our chaplains; Leon de Normanville, Richard of Musselburgh, John of Leicester, monks of Melrose; and many others.
